title: Pan-Seared Korean Pear Tagine
description: A sweet and savory dessert inspired by Moroccan flavors, featuring seared Korean pear in a spicy tagine sauce.
instructions: Peel and core the Korean pear, then slice into 1-inch wedges.
In a small saucepan, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat. Add the tagine sauce and bring to a simmer.
Add the honey and sumac (if using) to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Add the pear wedges to the saucepan and toss to coat with the tagine sauce.
Transfer the pear to a baking dish and dot with the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the pear is tender and caramelized.
Remove from the oven and garnish with sesame seeds (if using).
Serve warm, with additional tagine sauce for drizzling if desired.
